metadata.archetype_justification: No backend services, no database connections, no HTTP clients, no write operations, and no user-specific state management detected. All pages are static Angular component showcases with no server interaction. Classified as stateless-utility.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, or any infrastructure definitions
top_gaps.impact: Cannot deploy reproducibly; all infrastructure would need to be manually created

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No compute infrastructure defined — no ECS, EKS, Lambda, or EC2 definitions
top_gaps.impact: Application has no deployment target; cannot scale or run in the cloud

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: CI pipeline runs build checks only; no deploy stages, no environment promotion
top_gaps.impact: Cannot continuously deliver changes to production

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No deployment strategy — no blue/green, canary, or any deployment mechanism

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, DAST, or dependency scanning in CI/CD pipeline
top_gaps.impact: Vulnerabilities in dependencies or code reach production undetected

findings.title: No compute infrastructure defined
findings.description: No compute infrastructure is defined anywhere in the repository. There are no Terraform resources, no CloudFormation templates, no CDK stacks, no Kubernetes manifests, and no Dockerfile. The application is a pure source-code repository with a build artifact output (dist/) but no defined deployment target.
findings.gap: No compute infrastructure exists — the application cannot be deployed to any cloud environment without manual setup.
findings.recommendation: Define compute infrastructure using AWS CDK (TypeScript). For a static Angular application, the simplest approach is S3 + CloudFront. For containerized deployment aligned with preferences, use Amazon EKS with a Nginx-based container serving static assets.

findings.title: No network security controls defined
findings.description: No network infrastructure is defined. No VPC, subnets, security groups, NACLs, or any network configuration exists in the repository.
findings.gap: No network security controls are in place because no infrastructure is defined.
findings.recommendation: When defining deployment infrastructure, ensure the application is served via CloudFront with origin access control to a private S3 bucket, or deploy containers in a VPC with private subnets and a load balancer in public subnets.

findings.title: No API entry point or CDN defined
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront distribution, or any entry point is defined. The application has no defined way to receive traffic from the internet.
findings.gap: No managed entry point exists for serving the application to users.
findings.recommendation: Deploy with Amazon CloudFront as the entry point, providing CDN caching, HTTPS termination, and DDoS protection. Alternatively, use API Gateway as a frontend to the containerized application.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists because no compute infrastructure is defined.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling mechanism — if deployed manually, the application cannot respond to traffic changes.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to EKS, configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) based on CPU/request metrics. If using S3 + CloudFront, auto-scaling is inherent.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code coverage
findings.description: Zero infrastructure is defined in code. No Terraform files, no CloudFormation templates, no CDK stacks, no Helm charts exist in the repository.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage — no infrastructure is defined in any IaC format.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS CDK (TypeScript) to define all infrastructure — hosting (S3 + CloudFront or EKS), networking, DNS, and monitoring. CDK's TypeScript alignment reduces context switching.

findings.title: CI/CD has no deployment stages
findings.description: A GitHub Actions workflow exists for build verification across 3 platforms but has no deployment stages — no staging, no production deploy, no environment promotion.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is entirely manual. No deploy stage, no environment promotion, no release automation.
findings.recommendation: Extend the GitHub Actions workflow with deployment stages: build → test → deploy to staging → E2E tests → deploy to production. Use OIDC-based AWS authentication.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: The application uses Angular Router with hash-based URL routing. Routes are unversioned — changes to route structure would break any external links or bookmarks.
findings.gap: No versioning strategy for the application's URL routes or any externally-facing interface.
findings.recommendation: If the template evolves to include backend API endpoints, implement URL-path versioning (/v1/, /v2/) from the start.

findings.title: No audit logging configured
findings.description: No CloudTrail, audit logging, or any logging infrastructure is defined. No IaC exists to configure any AWS logging services.
findings.gap: No audit logging configured — no CloudTrail, no log retention, no immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: When deploying infrastructure, enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock for immutable storage.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: No API authentication exists. Login and register pages are purely visual templates with empty component classes — no authentication logic, no token handling, no OAuth2/JWT integration.
findings.gap: No authentication mechanism of any kind — login/register pages are non-functional visual templates.
findings.recommendation: When building a real application from this template, integrate Amazon Cognito for authentication. Implement JWT token validation with Angular HTTP interceptors and route guards.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No identity provider integration exists. Login/register pages are visual templates only with no connection to any IdP (Cognito, Okta, Auth0, or custom).
findings.gap: No centralized identity integration — application has no authentication at all.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as the centralized IdP. Configure Cognito User Pools with appropriate policies and MFA.

findings.title: No compute hardening or patching strategy
findings.description: No compute resources exist to harden or patch. No Dockerfile with base image selection, no AMI references, no SSM configuration, no vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: No compute hardening strategy — no base images, no patching automation, no vulnerability scanning for the runtime environment.
findings.recommendation: When containerizing, use a hardened base image (Bottlerocket for EKS nodes, distroless/nginx-alpine for the container). Enable Amazon Inspector for vulnerability scanning.

findings.title: No application security pipeline
findings.description: No security scanning tools are integrated into the CI/CD pipeline. The GitHub Actions workflow runs only npm ci, Playwright install, and npm run build. No SAST, no dependency scanning, no container scanning.
findings.gap: No security scanning in the pipeline — vulnerabilities in npm dependencies could go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot configuration (.github/dependabot.yml) for automated dependency updates. Add npm audit as a pipeline step. Consider adding Semgrep for SAST.

findings.title: No distributed tracing instrumented
findings.description: No distributed tracing is instrumented. No OpenTelemetry SDK, no X-Ray SDK, no trace ID propagation. Even frontend observability (Real User Monitoring) is absent.
findings.gap: No tracing or RUM instrumented — no visibility into user experience or request flows.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS CloudWatch RUM or OpenTelemetry browser instrumentation for frontend observability.

findings.title: No business metrics published
findings.description: No custom metrics are published. No CloudWatch metric calls, no analytics integration, no business event tracking.
findings.gap: No business metrics — no visibility into how the application is used or performing from a business perspective.
findings.recommendation: When deployed, integrate CloudWatch RUM for frontend performance metrics. Add custom metrics for key user interactions.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No alerting or anomaly detection is configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no error rate monitoring, no latency tracking.
findings.gap: No alerting — degradation or errors would go unnoticed.
findings.recommendation: When deployed, configure CloudWatch alarms for CloudFront error rates, latency, and origin health. Enable anomaly detection on key metrics.

findings.title: No deployment strategy exists
findings.description: No deployment strategy exists. The CI/CD pipeline has no deploy stage. There is no blue/green, canary, rolling update, or any deployment mechanism defined.
findings.gap: No deployment strategy — no safe way to release changes to production.
findings.recommendation: Implement blue/green deployment using CloudFront with origin switching or EKS with Argo Rollouts for canary deployments.

findings.title: No integration or E2E tests in CI
findings.description: Unit tests exist and are executed as part of the build. Playwright is installed but no E2E tests or integration tests run in the CI pipeline.
findings.gap: No integration or E2E tests run in CI despite Playwright being available. Unit tests only.
findings.recommendation: Add Playwright E2E tests that verify critical user journeys. Add a test execution step in GitHub Actions after the build.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident response automation, or self-healing mechanisms exist.
findings.gap: No incident response capability — all incidents would be handled ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: When deployed, create runbooks for common scenarios and define SSM Automation documents for automated remediation.

findings.title: No observability ownership defined
findings.description: No observability ownership is defined. No CODEOWNERS for monitoring configs, no per-service dashboards, no alarm ownership.
findings.gap: No observability ownership structure — no dashboards, no alarm owners, no monitoring accountability.
findings.recommendation: When deployed, establish observability ownership: create CloudWatch dashboards per environment, define alarm owners.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resources exist to tag. No IaC defines any resources, so no tagging strategy or enforcement can be evaluated.
findings.gap: No tagging strategy — when infrastructure is created, resources will lack cost allocation, ownership, and environment tags.
findings.recommendation: When defining IaC, implement a tagging strategy from the start using default_tags in Terraform or CDK Tags.of() for mandatory tags.

evaluations.reason: Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — no multi-step workflows exist
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — synchronous HTTP is correct design
Surface-gated: no persistent state to back up
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — sync request/response is correct design
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — no operations exceed 30 seconds
Surface-gated: no API surface or inter-service communication

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2 = 4 meets threshold; pathway not needed

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 1 (no compute infrastructure); no container definitions found in repository

triggering_questions.note: No compute infrastructure defined — no ECS, EKS, Lambda, or EC2

detail.current_state: No compute infrastructure of any kind is defined. No Dockerfile, docker-compose, or Kubernetes manifests exist.
immediate_actions: Create multi-stage Dockerfile (Node.js build → Nginx serve)

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected; DATA-Q4 = 4; no proprietary SQL

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures or proprietary SQL; threshold not met

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No persistent data store exists (surface-gated); INF-Q2 Not Evaluated

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — no persistent data store in this frontend-only application

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist; no streaming/ETL/analytics artifacts found
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No evidence of data processing workloads. The application is a static frontend with no streaming, ETL, or analytics infrastructure.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 = 1 (no IaC), INF-Q11 = 2 (partial CI/CD), OPS-Q5 = 1 (no deployment strategy), OPS-Q6 = 2 (limited testing)

triggering_questions.note: Build automated but no deployment stages
Unit tests only, no E2E in pipeline

detail.current_state: Zero IaC, CI/CD limited to build verification, no deployment stages, no E2E testing in pipeline.
immediate_actions: Add deployment stages to GitHub Actions
Add Playwright E2E test execution as pipeline gate

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'CoreUI Angular admin dashboard template' with no AI-related signal terms.
